articles.title: FIGMA RENDERING: POWERED BY WEBGPU
articles.summary: Figma upgraded its rendering engine from WebGL to WebGPU to unlock new performance optimizations, requiring significant changes to its graphics interface and shader processing systems. The transition involved creating explicit draw-call arguments, implementing uniform buffer batching, and building a dynamic fallback system to handle device compatibility issues. After extensive testing across multiple device types and careful rollout monitoring, Figma successfully deployed WebGPU while maintaining performance baselines and enabling future optimizations like compute shaders.

articles.title: FRAMER DESIGN PAGES
articles.summary: Framer's Design Pages transforms the platform from a web builder into a comprehensive design tool with features like vector editing, P3 colors, and advanced masking. The new freeform canvas allows designers to experiment without worrying about responsiveness, then convert iterations into web pages with one click. Design Pages are completely free with unlimited projects and pages, and integrate with Wireframer for generating layouts.

articles.title: CHROME IS GOOGLE'S FIRST IPHONE APP WITH LIQUID GLASS
articles.summary: Google Chrome 141 for iOS introduced Liquid Glass design updates, including a refreshed Tab Grid, modernized context menus, rounded corners in sheets and settings, a new pinwheel-style tab loader, and a floating keyboard shortcut bar. The changes highlight Chrome's unique iOS interface. Most other Google apps remain split between older Material Theme and newer Material 3 designs.

articles.title: RIP DESIGN AGENCY
articles.summary: AI has fundamentally broken the traditional design agency model by enabling cheap, fast, and high-quality work simultaneously for the first time. The proposed "Anti-Design Agency" approach embraces AI tools, works transparently in public, and charges lower prices due to increased efficiency. Traditional agencies that ignore AI will become irrelevant as designers who leverage AI can deliver faster results at more accessible price points.

articles.title: THE RIGHT SIZE PROJECT
articles.summary: Freelancers and small agencies should focus on "right-sized" projects rather than chasing big jobs they're not ready for. The key is delivering 10× ROI by charging clients 10% of the value created—for example, charging $1,000 for work that generates $10,000 in revenue or savings. Success comes from starting with smaller projects where you can guarantee outsized results, then gradually scaling up as you build proof and confidence in delivering larger value.

articles.title: WELCOME TO THE ENTRY-LEVEL VOID: WHAT HAPPENS WHEN JUNIOR DESIGN JOBS DISAPPEAR?
articles.summary: Entry-level design jobs are disappearing as AI, budget cuts, and remote work erode traditional apprenticeships, leaving newcomers to rely on unpaid gigs, cold outreach, and self-driven projects. What emerges is an uneven system where only the well-connected or persistent succeed, while AI accelerates learning but risks weakening creative foundations—pushing originality, peer networks, and self-started paths to replace the old career ladder.

articles.title: GPS FOR COLOR
articles.summary: Volume turns color selection from a flat swatch hunt into a sculptural, 3D design journey. Explore, build, and manipulate color structures as tangible objects and reveal hidden harmonies no flat picker can show.

articles.title: COPY AND PASTE THE MOST TRENDING COMPONENTS
articles.summary: Aceternity UI is a comprehensive collection of stunning effects ready to use for your website. It's shadcn/ui for magic effects.

articles.title: REACT BITS
articles.summary: An open source collection of animated, interactive, and fully customizable React components for building memorable websites.

articles.title: CHAMPIONING QUALITY THROUGH CULTURE
articles.summary: Quality emerges from well-designed systems and processes rather than being added at the end of projects. When systems enforce excellence and collaboration, teams can move quickly without sacrificing standards because clarity becomes built into every checkpoint. Building this culture requires long-term investment in feedback loops and accountability, making quality a natural byproduct rather than a competing priority with speed.

articles.title: DESIGNING LOOPS, NOT PATHS
articles.summary: Traditional workflow-based design is too rigid for real-world unpredictability, while loop-based systems use continuous feedback to sense, decide, act, and adapt. By keeping humans in the loop and enabling nested layers of oversight, these systems build resilience, foster trust, and support smarter, more flexible collaboration between people and AI.

articles.title: "SOME UK BRANDS ARE DEFINITELY TIRED," KOTO'S JAMES GREENFIELD ON THE STATE OF BRANDING TODAY
articles.summary: Koto's James Greenfield argues that strong branding requires discipline, distinctiveness, and context—work should feel “uncopyable,” evolve or revolutionize depending on a brand's state, and reflect real consumer experiences. From lessons in Amazon's refresh to critiques of UK legacy brands, he stresses that creativity with attitude, long-term thinking, and storytelling rooted in culture and business realities are what make brands memorable and trusted.

articles.title: BEN WOOD IS A DESIGNER DEDICATED TO GIVING HIS REJECTED AND UNFINISHED WORK A NEW LEASE OF LIFE
articles.summary: Ben Wood transforms his rejected and unfinished client work into a vibrant poster series called Untitled Unfinished, showcasing experimental typography and bold color choices.

articles.title: TRANSFORMING INDUSTRIAL LEGACY INTO URBAN INTERFACE
articles.summary: Munich's Werksviertel transforms an industrial district south of Ostbahnhof into a diverse urban quarter that preserves existing structures while adding new programs.

articles.title: THE MOST POWERFUL WEAPON IN A FREELANCER'S TOOLKIT ISN'T TALENT. IT'S COMMUNICATION
articles.summary: Creative freelancers succeed less from raw talent than from consistent, respectful communication.
